Hello Ana,

 

Yes, we will likely integrate with PluginHive, but my question to Sarah and then to your customer support via Sarah, was as follows:

 

''Apologies I am after some advice and information for Luke? Luke is in the process of trying to implement the rates into his website and wondered if there was any way he can apply the standard shipping rate and build in the additional surcharges such as fuel and remote area etc- is there any way that he can do this? How do customers tend to get around this in your experience?

 

Also if Luke was to look at an API and integration of live rates with say shopify, would these include the surcharges such as fuel, remote area etc automatically?''

 

I don't seem to have seen any clarification on this, or am I missing something? 

 

So basically, I am looking to implement the rates as agreed with DHL (Sarah) but I need to know all the additional charges and surcharges involved to get a final price. Does this make sense?

 

So if shipping is 0-5kg at £15.00, it's not £15.00 as there will be additional charges. I need to know all these charges so I can include them. I hope this makes sense?

 

Maybe your API includes these surcharges (Live) Maybe you can confirm this as I wouldn't know? Also, if it does that's great, but we still need a breakdown of what we are going to be charged as the shipping rates alone, as we all know, could go from for exmpale; £15.00 to £28 once you've put all additonals on top. I hope I am making sense - not sure what else to say, happy to chat on phone if needed :)

Regards,

 

Luke Mason
Managing Director

 
 

keylibrary.co.uk (https://keylibrary.co.uk/)

comments.body:
Hello Luke,

Thank you for the screenshot — that helps clarify exactly what you are seeing.

On the Tax ID and EORI question: the Tax ID field visible in the recipient address panel is used to pass the recipient's VAT number to DHL. Currently, the app does not display a separate EORI field for the consignee/recipient at the order level — only the single Tax ID field is available there. Your own EORI as the UK exporter (shipper side) should be entered in your From Address settings in the app under App Settings → From Address, which is where DHL picks it up for the export declaration. If you need to pass both a VAT number and an EORI for the recipient on each shipment, you can provide the it in the Tax ID field as DHL only provides one Tax ID field via API for the recipient.

On adjusting package weight: yes, you can manually edit the total weight before generating a label. When you open an order in the app, the Packaging Summary section has an edit option that lets you override the weight — you can use this to include the box and packaging material rather than just the item weights.

On extra insurance: DHL Express does support declared value/insurance on shipments. For specific orders, you can enable the Insurance in the app as in attached screenshot.

Let us know if you have any further queries.

Thanks and regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

Hello Luke,
 
Thank you for the screenshot — that helps clarify exactly what you are seeing.
 
On the Tax ID and EORI question: the Tax ID field visible in the recipient address panel is used to pass the recipient's VAT number to DHL. Currently, the app does not display a separate EORI field for the consignee/recipient at the order level — only the single Tax ID field is available there. Your own EORI as the UK exporter (shipper side) should be entered in your From Address settings in the app under App Settings → From Address, which is where DHL picks it up for the export declaration. If you need to pass both a VAT number and an EORI for the recipient on each shipment, you can provide the it in the Tax ID field as DHL only provides one Tax ID field via API for the recipient.
 
On adjusting package weight: yes, you can manually edit the total weight before generating a label. When you open an order in the app, the Packaging Summary section has an edit option that lets you override the weight — you can use this to include the box and packaging material rather than just the item weights.
 
On extra insurance: DHL Express does support declared value/insurance on shipments. For specific orders, you can enable the Insurance in the app as in attached screenshot.
 
Let us know if you have any further queries. 
 
Thanks and regards,
Touseef

Hello Luke Mason,

Once the Royal Mail account is connected, we can set automation rules to determine which zone the Royal Mail account applies.

Royal Mail integration via EasyPost is easy to set up. You will be connecting your Royal Mail OBA account through EasyPost and using it to generate shipping labels.

**Step 1:** Sign up for an EasyPost account.

**Step 3:** Generate the EasyPost production API key.
**Step 4:** Connect your EasyPost account in the app by navigating to **App > Settings > Add Carrier**.

Hello Luke,

Based on the screenshots you shared, you have already found the right option: Royal Mail Rates (the one marked NEW in the app). This uses the public rate card, requires no API credentials, and does not include label generation — which matches your setup perfectly.

This means Royal Mail services will appear at checkout for your EU and international customers while remaining hidden for UK orders, where your existing UK rates will continue to show as normal.

Hello Luke,

The condition to use in the automation rule is 'Destination Country', not 'Zone' — that is why the entry field is not behaving as expected. Here is how to set it up correctly:

1. In the app, go to Settings → Shipping Rates → Rate Automation and create a new rule.
2. For the condition, select 'Destination Country' from the condition type dropdown.
3. Set the operator to 'is not equal to' (or 'does not include').
4. From the country selector that appears, choose 'United Kingdom'.
5. For the action, set it to show Royal Mail Rates services for orders matching that condition.

This will display Royal Mail options at checkout for all non-UK destinations while keeping your existing UK rates unchanged.
